title: [CI:DOCS] Manpage syntax proposal
body:
I looked at the man pages and found that while they are consistent in their basic concept, many things concerning formatting are not.
For example, it is not consistent that options are behind an H4 header.
The biggest criticism, however, is how commands and options are handled when referencing them in a text.
There is no clear structure between the man pages regarding this. Sometimes backticks are used and sometimes they are written in italic.
Also, the formatting regarding the appearance of the commands is not consistent either.
I would like to propose a uniform formatting and, if accepted, apply it to all man pages.
Uniformity is very important to me and it should be clear to the user when reading several man pages what exactly their content and references are about.

see also: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/10336#discussioncomment-767644

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
This sounds good to me.  Once we standardized formats here, we would also need to apply these defaults to Buildah, Skopeo, Containers/Image, Containers/Common and containers/storage man pages as well.

@vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
We should add a link to this in the `docs/README.md` and also the `CONTRIBUTING.md`.  The file name should be uppercased too to make it stand out better, i.e. `docs/MANPAGE_SYNTAX.md`.

@Procyhon I'd a number of nits through out, but the general concept is awesome!  TYVM for bringing this forward.  @edsantiago if you have  a moment to take a look, that would be great.  I've also asked Gabriela Necasova to take a look, but I unfortunately don't have her GitHub handle handy to tag her.  I've sent her a private e-mail.
@rhatdan I second your thought on also using the final solution across at least Buildah, Skopeo, and Common.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
FWIW, I passed this past Gabi Necasova too and she thought it looked great!
So my question, @rhatdan , is does this template belong in Podman or should we move it to c/common with links and add links from the other projects that we want to use this against?
It should eventually be in containers/common with links.
